latin: Sicut agnus coram tondente se obmutescet, et non aperiet os suum.
english: Like a lamb before its shearer, He shall be dumb, and shall not open His mouth.

latin: Horrenda prorsus impietas, quæ tantis injuriis satiari non potuit, quin potius ferali rabie fremens, impio judici, tamquam rabido cani, animam justi deglutiendam exposuit.
english: Horrible impiety indeed, which could not be sated with so many injuries, but rather, raging with savage madness, exposed the soul of the just One to the wicked judge, as to a mad dog, to be devoured.

latin: Da mihi misero te desiderare, desiderando quærere, quærendo invenire, inveniendo amare, amando mala mea redimere, redempta non iterare.
english: Grant to me, a wretch, to desire you; by desiring, to seek you; by seeking, to find you; by finding, to love you; by loving, to redeem my evils; and, once redeemed, to do them no more.

latin: Magna quippe licentia, et magna gratia est mittere manum in latus illud dulcissimum, in latus illud saluberrimum, in latus, inquam, unde ab ira salvamur, unde a culpa sanamur, unde in gratia regeneramur, unde in gloria sublimamur.
english: Great indeed is the liberty, and great the grace, to put one's hand into that sweetest side, that most healing side — into the side, I say, whence we are saved from wrath, whence we are healed of our guilt, whence we are reborn into grace, whence we are lifted up into glory.

latin: Tristis est anima mea usque ad mortem.
english: My soul is sorrowful unto death.

latin: Et ideo, aliis luminaribus exstinctis, una candela in officio tenebrarum servatur accensa, quia lumen fidei in sola remansit virgine Maria.
english: And therefore, when the other lights are extinguished, one candle is kept burning in the office of Tenebrae, because the light of faith remained in the Virgin Mary alone.

latin: Necessarium enim erit, ut aliquando ita cogites te praesentem cogitatione tua, ac si tunc temporis ibi praesens fuisses quando passus fuit.
english: For it will be needful that you sometimes so reckon yourself present in your thought, as though you had then and there been present when He suffered.

latin: Bene in igne Spiritus apparuit, quia ab omni corde quod replet, torporem frigoris excutit, et hoc in desiderio suæ æternitatis accendit.
english: Well did the Spirit appear in fire, for from every heart that He fills He shakes out the torpor of cold, and kindles it in the desire of His own eternity.
from: The Fire That Does Not Consume

latin: Fides ardebat; sed humana infirmitas in profundum trahebat.
english: His faith was burning; but human weakness was dragging him down into the deep.
from: On the Waves of the Night

latin: Plus itaque mihi contulit Deus redimendo, quam creando: creando quippe me mihi dedit, redimendo autem et seipsum dedit, et me mihi reddidit.
english: God gave me more in redeeming than in creating: for in creating He gave me to myself, but in redeeming He gave both Himself, and gave me back to myself.

latin: Ille languens, istæ tristes, omnes dilecti.
english: He was the one languishing, these two were grieving, and all of them were beloved.

latin: Domina igitur orante et lacrymas dulciter emittente, ecce subito venit Dominus Jesus in albissimis vestibus gloriae et Resurrectionis novitatis suae; vultu sereno, speciosus, gloriosus, et gaudens, ac Matri desolatae et moerenti totus festivus apparens.
english: And so, as our Lady was praying and sweetly pouring out her tears, behold, suddenly the Lord Jesus came in garments most white, of the glory and newness of His Resurrection; with serene face, beautiful, glorious, and rejoicing, and to His desolate and mourning Mother He appeared, all festal.
from: The First to See Him Risen

latin: Mulier, quæ fuerat janua mortis, prima prædicat resurrectionem, et ostendit januam vitæ.
english: The woman who had been the gate of death is the first to preach the Resurrection, and shows the gate of life.

latin: Conveniens enim fuit, ut verus flos, id est Christus, conciperetur in flore, id est, in Nazareth; et de flore, id est, de Beata Virgine; et cum floribus, id est, tempore florum.
english: For it was fitting that the true Flower, that is Christ, should be conceived in the flower, that is, in Nazareth; and of the flower, that is, of the Blessed Virgin; and with the flowers, that is, in the season of flowers.
from: The Word Made Flesh in Her

latin: O unicum bonum meum! O singulare gaudium meum! Quid faciam cum te sic videam?
english: O my one good! O my one and only joy! What shall I do, when I see You like this?

latin: Tunc vere possidebis terram, quando inhæseris ei, qui fecit cœlum et terram.
english: Then truly shall you possess the earth, when you cleave to Him who made heaven and earth.

latin: Sequere et tu pretiosissimum cœli terræque thesaurum; et porta vel pedes vel manus, brachiaque sustenta; terræ defluentes minutatim pretiosissimi sanguinis guttas curiosius collige; et pulverem pedum ejus linge.
english: Follow, you too, the most precious treasure of heaven and earth; carry either His feet or His hands, and bear up His arms; gather up with care the drops of most precious blood that fall little by little upon the ground; and kiss the dust of His feet.

latin: Stabat Maria levans brachia in altum; et dum tangere eum valuit, caput et manus ejus dependentia trahebat supra triste pectus suum.
english: Mary stood, lifting her arms on high; and when she could reach him, she drew his hanging head and hands down upon her sorrowing breast.

latin: Nos ergo, exemplo Christi et Joannis, agitemur a Spiritu Sancto, non per hypocrisim a maligno, in desertum loci et solitudinis, vel saltem in desertum nostri pectoris et contemplationis, ut quieti a strepitu mundi, melius Deo vacemus.
english: Let us then, after the example of Christ and of John, be driven by the Holy Spirit — and not, through hypocrisy, by the evil one — into the desert of place and solitude, or at the least into the desert of our own breast and of contemplation, that, resting from the noise of the world, we may better wait upon God.

latin: Quis ita dormit quando voluerit, sicut Jesus mortuus est, quando voluit?
english: Who so falls asleep when he wills, as Jesus died when He willed?

latin: Merito ab Oriente veniunt, qui Solem justitiæ novum nobis ortum annuntiant, lætisque rumoribus totum mundum illuminant.
english: Rightly do they come from the East, who proclaim to us the Sun of Justice newly risen, and with glad tidings fill the whole world with light.
from: The Star and the Three Kings

latin: Verbum æternum non nascitur, nisi in corde pacato; unde Psalmus dicit: Factus est in pace locus ejus.
english: The eternal Word is not born except in a heart at peace; whence the Psalm says: His place is made in peace.

latin: O quanta fiducia et fiducialis audacia, quod factura Factorem, creatura Creatorem, homo Deum Patrem audeat nuncupare!
english: O what confidence, what daring trust, that the thing made should dare to name its Maker, the creature its Creator, a man should dare to call God his Father!

latin: Radix amara crucis evanuit, flos vitæ cum fructibus erupit; et qui jacuit in morte, surrexit in gloria; sol post occasum oritur.
english: The bitter root of the cross has faded; the flower of life has burst forth with its fruits; and he who lay in death has risen in glory; the sun rises after its setting.
from: The New Day After the Sabbath

latin: Sic quotidie facit nobiscum spiritualiter: si enim aliqua perplexitate vel accidia gravati pie de ipso loquimur, statim adest, confortans corda nostra et illuminans, et etiam ad amorem suum inflammans.
english: So he does with us each day in spirit: for if, weighed down by some perplexity or listlessness, we speak of him devoutly, at once he is present, strengthening our hearts and enlightening them, and setting them aflame with love of himself.

latin: Quasi enim homo navem ascendit, sed quasi Deus mare conturbat; quasi homo in navi dormit, sed quasi Deus ventis et mari imperat, et eorum furorem verbo coercet.
english: For as man He boards the boat, but as God He troubles the sea; as man He sleeps in the boat, but as God He commands the winds and the sea, and restrains their fury by a word.
from: The Master Asleep in the Boat

latin: Ex quo universum conculcat tumorem, dum is qui exit a Deo, et ad Deum vadit, pedes lavare voluit.
english: By this he treads down all swelling pride, when he who comes from God and goes to God willed to wash men's feet.

latin: Sciens quod amanti sufficit solum insinuare necessitatem, absque ulla petitione.
english: Knowing that, for one who loves, it is enough only to hint at the need, without any petition at all.

latin: Domine Jesu Christe, veni in Naim, animam meam fluctuantem tentationibus.
english: Lord Jesus Christ, come into Nain, into my soul tossed by temptations.
